summary: A deceptively quiet week on paper — zero meetings, low email — but dominated by invisible life-transition load: apartment lease signed, large financial transfer detected, 14 admin tasks with 0% completion across all 7 days.

notes.meeting_load: 0 meeting hours scores 0 raw, but +5 for depletion-swing context after a 14-hour prior week
notes.task_backlog: 14 tasks x2 base (28) + 3 overdue x1.5 (4.5), rounded to 32; 0% completion applied as full-weight penalty
notes.financial_stress: large_transfer_detected=true triggers floor of 20; account setup complexity adds 5; spend_vs_budget_pct null (data gap) does not reduce score
notes.life_transition_surcharge: lease signing + financial account setup = cognitive overhead not captured in raw signals; +10 applied

insight_hooks: 14 admin tasks, 0 completed, 3 overdue — the task system was open all week but never used
Week cleared of all meetings after a 14-hour overloaded prior week — relief turned into paralysis, not recovery
Large financial transfer and lease signing represent real cognitive weight that does not appear in meeting or email metrics
Email volume dropped 66% (147 to 50) but 64% unread rate suggests passive inbox avoidance
The user had maximum theoretical focus time (8-hour blocks, 7 free evenings) but zero task output — structural avoidance, not capacity shortage

comparison_to_prior_week.interpretation: Surface signals improved dramatically; underlying load did not — the life transition transferred cognitive pressure from calendar to invisible admin paralysis

for_insight_writer.lead_with: The week looked free but felt heavy
for_insight_writer.core_tension: Maximum available time, zero task output — this is not a capacity problem
for_insight_writer.avoid: Framing as productivity failure; frame as transition overwhelm instead
for_insight_writer.financial_angle: Do not alarm about spend (data gap, $0 tracked) but acknowledge the large transfer as real stress even if the number is not visible
